proj-1773719443026.goal: The app has broken imports and exports across files. Go through every file, make sure named vs default exports match their imports, function call arguments match definitions, and only use packages that are in package.json. The project should compile and run cleanly.

allTasks.description: Update src/hooks/useGameLogic.js. 1. Bug Fix: Locate the increment function and remove the line calling setFloatingNumbers entirely, as App.jsx handles this separately to prevent duplicates. 2. Timer Feature: Introduce a new state variable elapsedSeconds using useState(0). 3. Timer Logic: Implement a useEffect hook that sets an interval (setInterval) to increment elapsedSeconds by 1 every 1000ms. Ensure the cleanup function clears the interval on unmount. 4. Exports: Ensure the returned object from useGameLogic explicitly includes elapsedSeconds, buildings state, and buyBuilding function alongside existing properties.

Update UI for GameFooter and formatting utilities. 1. In src/utils/formatNumber.js, add a new exported function formatTime(seconds) that takes elapsedSeconds and returns a string formatted as MM:SS (e.g., '05:30'). Handle padding with leading zeros. 2. In src/components/GameFooter.jsx, update the component to accept three new props: elapsedSeconds (number), buildings (array), and buyBuilding (function). 3. Replace the hardcoded '0:00' timer display with a call to formatTime(elapsedSeconds) imported from formatNumber.js. 4. Create a Shop section inside GameFooter that maps over the buildings array prop. For each building, render a clickable button displaying its name/upgrade info and calling buyBuilding(index) on click.

Wire up the App component to pass data to Footer. 1. In src/App.jsx, import useGameLogic from src/hooks/useGameLogic.js and formatTime is not needed here but ensure useGameLogic is called within the main App function. 2. Destructure elapsedSeconds, buildings, and buyBuilding from the useGameLogic hook return value. 3. Locate the <GameFooter /> component usage in the render method. 4. Pass elapsedSeconds, buildings, and buyBuilding as props to GameFooter to enable the timer display and shop functionality implemented in TASK-002.

fixes_needed.issue: ReferenceError: 'score' is not defined. The component uses 'score' to calculate progressPercent but it is not passed as a prop from App.jsx.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Add 'score' to the destructured props list in GameFooter. Then, update src/App.jsx to pass score={score} to the <GameFooter> component.

fixes_needed.issue: Duplicate Floating Numbers. The 'increment' function adds floating numbers, but App.jsx also calls 'spawnFloatingNumber'. This causes two visual effects per click.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: Remove the logic inside the 'increment' function that creates 'newNumber', calls 'setFloatingNumbers', and sets the timeout for removal. Floating number management should only happen in 'spawnFloatingNumber'.

fixes_needed.issue: Building Purchase Logic Mismatch. GameFooter passes the array index to buyBuilding, but useGameLogic expects the building 'id'. IDs start at 1, indices start at 0.
fixes_needed.fix_instruction: In the onClick handler for shop buttons, change buyBuilding(index) to buyBuilding(building.id).

_reviewResult.summary: The project structure is well-organized with clear separation of concerns between hooks and components. However, there are critical cross-file integration issues preventing the application from functioning correctly. The GameFooter component will crash due to a missing 'score' prop passed from App.jsx. Additionally, there is a logic mismatch in building purchases (index vs ID) causing incorrect behavior, and floating number effects are duplicated because both the hook's increment function and the App component trigger them.

global._currentProjectGoal: Create an "idle clicker game" similar to cookie clicker. You can choose the design and theme. Be as creative as you like. Use the reference photo as inspiration.

avalon-landing.goal: Build a modern landing page for Avalon Platforms™ — a premium cloud infrastructure company. Include: hero section with bold headline "Infrastructure Without Limits", features grid (3 cards: Global Edge Network, Zero-Downtime Deploys, AI-Powered Scaling), pricing section (3 tiers: Starter $29/mo, Pro $99/mo, Enterprise custom), testimonials carousel, and a contact form. Use a dark theme with electric blue (#0066FF) accents. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

avalon-v2.goal: Recreate this link-in-bio style landing page for Avalon Platforms™. Match the visual design from the reference URL exactly: full-screen video/image background with dark overlay, centered vertical layout, glassmorphism frosted-glass style buttons with semi-transparent backgrounds and subtle borders, the Avalon Platforms™ heading with tagline "Insanely simple marketing technologies", icon row, feature link buttons (Create stunning micro sites, Make incredible ads with AI, Fast & simple email marketing, Go to Avalon Platforms), Clone this site button, and email input with social icons at the bottom. Use the Geist font family. Dark/moody aesthetic with glass-effect UI elements. Tech stack: Next.js 14 App Router + Tailwind CSS.

idle-clicker.goal: Create an "idle clicker game" similar to cookie clicker. You can choose the design and theme. Be as creative as you like. Use the reference photo as inspiration.

_finalReview.summary: The core clicker loop functions well with proper state management via hooks and component separation, but there is a critical wiring issue where the 'score' prop is missing from GameFooter, breaking the progress bar visualization. Additionally, inline styles override CSS classes for floating numbers which reduces maintainability and performance.
suggestions: Fix Prop Wiring & State Logic: Update App.jsx to pass the 'score' state variable as a prop to GameFooter so the progress bar calculation works correctly. In useGameLogic.js, consider deriving cookiesPerSecond from the buildings array (count * cps) instead of maintaining it as separate state to prevent potential drift if building data changes.

UI Polish & Animation Refinement: Move the 'floatUp' animation logic entirely into index.css by removing inline style properties for animation and color in FloatingNumber.jsx, keeping only position coordinates inline. Add a visual active-state effect to CookieButton using CSS :active pseudo-class to improve tactile feedback when clicking.

Feature Expansion & Persistence: Implement localStorage saving/loading in useGameLogic.js so players don't lose progress on refresh by serializing the buildings and totalCookies state. Additionally, expand the initial buildings array with 2-3 new types (e.g., 'Mine', 'Factory') that have higher costs and CPS values to extend gameplay longevity.
